sourceMovie.title: Going in Style
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Going in Style (2017) stuck because it commits to its crime premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Zach Braff's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The crime and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Old Man & the Gun
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Going in Style clicked for you, The Old Man & the Gun (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Going in Style?
faq.answer: Try The Old Man & the Gun, The Heist of the Century, Small Time Crooks—each shares DNA with Going in Style while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Going in Style?
faq.answer: Going in Style (2017) is best described as Crime, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Going in Style worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
